detailedExemplarData.title: Finding the area of the Earth visible from a given height

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Exceptionally clear organization with professional headings, sub-headings, numbered equations and labelled figures
Highly coherent structure: introduction, body and conclusion are well demarcated and easy to navigate
All content directly supports the investigation with minimal irrelevant material
weaknesses: Minor digressions (e.g. lengthy proof of the surfaceâarea integral and humorous dog imagery) slightly reduce conciseness
Some reference URLs have inconsistent formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there are a few nonâessential asides, the reportâs structure is exemplary by IA standards. Headings and visual layout enable quick navigation, and nearly all material is relevant. The logical flow from motivation through modelling to evaluation is maintained throughout. These qualities place it firmly in the top band for Presentation.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of calculus notation throughout (implicit differentiation, integral notation)
Effective use of multiple representations: algebraic derivations, Desmos graphs, schematic diagrams and summary tables
Well-structured mathematical arguments with clear logical progression and equation referencing
weaknesses: Occasional lapses such as âdxÂ²â and minor limitâargument informality
Some paragraphs remain overly dense and could be broken into subpoints
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used correctly almost without fail, and the variety of representations effectively supports the investigation. The logical structure is very clear, with only minor slips preventing a perfect score. Overall, the communication meets the highest band criteria.

strengths: Strong personal motivation grounded in drone photography and a playful dog-observer narrative
Creative reorientation of the model onto the x-axis showing personal initiative
Demonstrates independent thinking in deriving the horizon formula rather than quoting a standard result
weaknesses: The investigative route, while personal, follows conventional calculus techniques
Greater depth of innovative method or nonâstandard approach would be needed for further distinction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is highly personalized and shows clear creativity and initiative beyond the expected. The studentâs own context drives the inquiry, and independent derivations are undertaken. These aspects satisfy the top band descriptors for Personal Engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection appears early (modelling assumptions) and continues through sanity checks and result comparisons
Deep analysis of implications (e.g. Earth oblateness, viewing-angle variable)
Comprehensive evaluation with suggested future improvements and real-world checks against NASA data
weaknesses: Some reflective commentary could be more tightly integrated with each mathematical section
A few insights (e.g. geometric intuition behind counter-intuitive results) are noted but not fully expanded
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection permeates the report: the student critically examines modelling choices, verifies results against known values, and proposes substantive refinements. The depth and consistency of this analysis align with the highest band for Reflection.

strengths: Expert application of HL-level calculus (implicit differentiation, surface-area integrals, limit arguments)
Mathematics is integral and used comprehensively to resolve the research question
Advanced techniques (Mean Value Theorem justification, Riemann-sum arguments) are invoked even if informally at times
weaknesses: Informal âdxÂ²â proposition is conceptually flawed and should be replaced by a formal Riemann-sum argument
Computations for only two of six heights are given fullyâcompleting all would strengthen the scope
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics drives the entire investigation and is applied expertly at and beyond SL level. Implicit differentiation, derivation of surface-area integrals and limit processes are handled with sophistication. Despite minor lapses, the work exemplifies the highest standard of Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are structured into short logical steps; equations are referenced and transitions between steps are explained, giving good clarity.  Occasional over-long paragraphs and the questionable proof about dxÂ² lower the standard from âexcellentâ to âgoodâ logical communication.

Most calculations are correct and the final formula agrees with known results, indicating good understanding.  Nevertheless, the informal handling of dxÂ² and the claim that certain integrals âevaluate to Î¸â reveal conceptual gaps, so complete accuracy is not demonstrated.

The student employs algebraic working, labelled graphs from Desmos, schematic diagrams and a summary table of results.  These representations complement each other effectively but are not especially sophisticated or varied beyond the expected set, so the work fits the âeffectiveâ category.

The exploration flows from a personal motivation, through modelling, derivation, results and evaluation in a sequence that is easy to follow.  Each section builds naturally on the previous one (e.g. 2-D model â surface-of-revolution derivation â application).  However, some digressions (e.g. extended derivation of the surface-area formula and an informal âdxÂ² = 0â argument) momentarily interrupt the line of reasoning, so the development is not consistently excellent.

Using a playful narrative (dog observer) and constructing a bespoke general surface-area derivation illustrate creativity, yet the overall method is standard calculus; thus the work shows a good, but not exceptional, degree of initiative.

The student comments on why half the Earth becomes the limit area, verifies extreme-case behaviour and notes potential errors from Earthâs oblateness, showing thoughtful but not deep critical analysis.

Outcomes are checked against the known surface area of Earth and possible improvements (introducing a viewing-angle variable) are suggested, giving a clear evaluation with some implications, but not the comprehensive treatment expected for the top band.

Reflection appears chiefly in the conclusion where the student discusses two modelling assumptions and their implications.  This constitutes meaningful but not extensive reflection integrated âthroughoutâ the report.

Standard calculus notation, implicit differentiation and integral notation are used almost all the time.  Minor slips such as writing an integral with a second differential âdxÂ²â and a casual treatment of limits show inconsistency, so the performance is âmostlyâ rather than âfullyâ accurate.

The investigation grows out of the studentâs own drone-photography experience, and personal decisions (e.g. re-orienting the coordinate system) are evident.  This demonstrates a clear personal approach though it remains within conventional mathematical techniques.

Most material directly supports the investigation, but there are sections that are more elaborate than required (e.g. a full proof of the surface area integral and humorous dog imagery).  These additions reduce conciseness even though they do not derail relevance, justifying the âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ band.

Calculus of tangents, implicit differentiation, surface area of revolution and limit evaluations are all relevant and applied to solve the stated problem comprehensively.  The mathematics extends across multiple sections and is integral to the investigation.

Deriving the horizon formula independently and choosing an analytical route rather than quoting a known result show significant independent thinking, but not the depth or originality required for the highest band.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered equations and labelled figures give the report a clear introduction, body and conclusion.  The reader can locate any stage of the argument quickly and the visual layout is professional; therefore the organization is exceptional for IA standards.

All mathematics is solidly at HL level (implicit differentiation, derivation of a surface-area integral, limit arguments).  The techniques are used appropriately and purposefully, matching the course expectations; however, they are not exceptionally advanced to merit the expert (6-mark) band.

annotations.comment: The student effectively grounds the investigation in a personal drone scenario, demonstrating motivation and engagement with real-world context.
The humorous dog image, while engaging, distracts from the mathematical focus. Consider replacing or minimizing it to improve conciseness.
Acknowledging that the Earth model is a perfect sphere introduces important reflection on error sources early in the report.
The long single paragraph describing the model is dense. Breaking it into shorter sentences with clear subpoints would enhance clarity and readability.
The step explaining why the circle of latitude bounds the visible region is critical. Clarify this geometric reasoning to strengthen logical flow.
The affine intercepts câ and câ are stated as r+ but omit h. Correct notation to câ=câ=r+h to ensure mathematical accuracy.
Explain succinctly why mâ=âmâ by symmetry before introducing the intercepts to maintain logical structure in the derivation.
The remark that the simplicity contradicts intuition is valuable; consider expanding on the geometric rationale behind this result for deeper reflection.
Reorienting the model onto the x-axis showcases creativity in handling inverse functions, demonstrating personal initiative.
The algebraic simplification to Aâ=2ÏrÂ²h/(r+h) is concise and accurate, demonstrating strong mathematical application.
The implicit differentiation to obtain dy/dx=âx/y is clear and correctly executed, demonstrating strong use of calculus techniques.
The derivation yielding y= rÂ²/(r+h) is handled smoothly, showing accurate algebraic manipulation of the tangency condition.
In Section 4, clearly define all variables (râ,râ,s) before invoking the frustum formula to improve the flow of mathematical communication.
The generalization of surface area via approximated frusta is conceptually sound and uses multiple representations effectively.
When applying the Mean Value Theorem, briefly justify why f(Î¾áµ¢)âf(xáµ¢) holds in the limit to strengthen rigor.
Including a formal statement of the Mean Value Theorem enhances mathematical communication and shows understanding of underlying theorems.
The informal âPropositionâ arguing dxÂ² integrates to Î¸ is conceptually flawed. Remove this and rely on formal Riemannâsum arguments instead.
The correction using dominance to eliminate the second integral is effective; the standard result A=â«2Ïfâ{1+fâ²Â²}dx is clearly recovered.
The derivative of f(x)=â(rÂ²âxÂ²) is computed accurately, demonstrating strong use of HL-level calculus.
The sanity check yielding At=4ÏrÂ² is an excellent reflection step, confirming the derivation with known formulae.
Only two of the six listed heights have computed values. Complete calculations for all entries to fully realize the investigationâs scope.
Ensure consistent formatting of units and numerical presentation (e.g. kmÂ² vs km^2 and use of commas) throughout the results section.
Comparing computed areas to NASAâs total surface area shows thoughtful critical analysis of results.
The conclusion identifies key assumptions (viewing angle, Earth shape) and proposes clear improvements, reflecting depth in evaluation.
Some URLs in the references contain line breaks and inconsistent styling. Standardize reference formatting for enhanced presentation.
